title: T-040 — NCR outbox events + permission strings (quality.ncr.*)
prompt:
# T-040 — NCR outbox events + permission strings

PRD: §12.1 P1 INTEGRATIONS consumer-only, §13.3 audit log completeness, §7.1 Epic 8D 09-d-02

## Goal
Wire 6 typed quality.ncr.* outbox events into NCR Server Actions; finalize quality.ncr.* permission enum; stub 05-WH consumer test for critical-close → hold-suggestion contract.

## Implementation contract
1. packages/events/src/quality/ncr.ts exports 6 typed event interfaces + Zod validators.
2. NCR Server Actions (T-038) emit events via packages/outbox publisher with idempotency key = ncr_id+status+seq.
3. packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts gains 8 quality.ncr.* permissions.
4. Test stub in apps/web/src/server/quality/ncr/__tests__/outbox.test.ts asserts critical-close → 05-WH hold-suggestion contract (the consumer impl lives in 05-WH module).

## Files
- packages/events/src/quality/ncr.ts [create]
- packages/events/src/index.ts [modify]
- apps/web/src/server/quality/ncr/actions.ts [modify]
- apps/web/src/server/quality/ncr/__tests__/outbox.test.ts [create]
- packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. 6 event types exported with Zod validators + required fields
2. closeNcr emits quality.ncr.closed once, deduped by idempotency key
3. critical close emits an event that the 05-WH stub consumer turns into a hold-suggestion row (in this test the consumer is mocked; real impl in 05-WH)
4. 8 quality.ncr.* permissions present in enum

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: outbox.test.ts asserting all 4 AC paths before code
- Run 'pnpm --filter events build && pnpm --filter web test ncr/outbox && pnpm --filter rbac test'

## Risk red lines
- Use canonical 6 event names (audit A §1)
- Enforce idempotency keys (Foundation FT-024)
- Reserve permission strings in packages/rbac canonical enum (audit B §3 red-line)

pipeline_inputs.description: Wire quality.ncr.* outbox events (created/submitted/assigned/status_changed/closed/reopened) into NCR Server Actions, with a typed contract in packages/events and consumer test stubs for 12-REPORTING and 05-WH.
pipeline_inputs.details: packages/events/src/quality/ncr.ts declares typed event shapes for quality.ncr.created, quality.ncr.submitted, quality.ncr.assigned, quality.ncr.status_changed, quality.ncr.closed, quality.ncr.reopened. Each event carries {tenant_id (org_id), ncr_id, ncr_number, severity, status, prior_status?, actor_user_id, signature_hash?, occurred_at}. NCR Server Actions (T-038) emit via packages/outbox publisher with idempotency key = ncr_id+status+seq. quality.ncr.closed with severity='critical' triggers a 05-WH hold-suggestion consumer (verified by stub test: GIVEN event published THEN warehouse hold queue receives suggestion). quality.ncr.* event types added to the outbox channel registry. Permission strings (extends T-038 reservation, finalized here): quality.ncr.create, quality.ncr.submit, quality.ncr.assign, quality.ncr.update_status, quality.ncr.close, quality.ncr.close.critical, quality.ncr.read, quality.ncr.export. PRD anchors verified 2026-05-14: §12.1 P1 INTEGRATIONS table lists outbound NCR events as consumer-only (no outbound stage in P1 — outbox is internal cross-module bus, not external API); §13.3 audit log completeness drives the audit-row pairing; §7.1 Epic 8D 09-d-02 scope.
out_of_scope: Do not implement 05-WH consumer here (cross-module, owned by 05-WH)
Do not build outbound HTTP webhooks (P2 INTEGRATIONS stage)
Do not modify outbox worker (T-040 ops-related is Foundation FT-040)
acceptance_criteria: Given the events typed contract package, when TypeScript build runs, then packages/events exports QualityNcrCreatedEvent, QualityNcrSubmittedEvent, QualityNcrAssignedEvent, QualityNcrStatusChangedEvent, QualityNcrClosedEvent, QualityNcrReopenedEvent, each with required tenant_id, ncr_id, ncr_number, actor_user_id, occurred_at fields and Zod runtime validator

Given a closeNcr call settles successfully, when outbox is inspected, then 1 quality.ncr.closed row exists with severity, signature_hash, and the idempotency key = ncr_id+'closed'+seq; a second close attempt on the same NCR yields the same key and is deduplicated

Given a quality.ncr.closed event with severity='critical' is published, when the 05-WH consumer stub receives it, then it inserts a hold-suggestion row tied to the original NCR reference_id; the test asserts the suggestion row, not actual hold creation

Given packages/rbac, when permissions enum is loaded, then quality.ncr.create, quality.ncr.submit, quality.ncr.assign, quality.ncr.update_status, quality.ncr.close, quality.ncr.close.critical, quality.ncr.read, quality.ncr.export are present (8 permissions)
test_strategy: RED: apps/web/src/server/quality/ncr/__tests__/outbox.test.ts asserting 6 event types emitted on respective actions + idempotency + critical→hold-suggestion stub + 8 permissions present before implementation
Run 'pnpm --filter events build && pnpm --filter web test ncr/outbox && pnpm --filter rbac test'
risk_red_lines: Do not invent new event names — use the 6 quality.ncr.* canonical set per audit A §1 cross-module event contract pin
Do not skip idempotency key (outbox dedup contract — Foundation FT-024)
Do not bypass packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts (audit B §3 red-line: permission strings reserved in one canonical surface)
Do not couple 05-WH consumer details to the event shape — events MUST be addressed by tenant_id + ncr_id only; warehouse decides how to act
